name: New Quay to Cwmtydu (Ceredigion Coast)
terrain: Cliff path, stiles, steep in places
highlights: Dolphin-watching cliff walk past smugglers' coves to a secluded seal beach.
points_of_interest: Bottlenose dolphin hotspot; grey seal pupping cove at Cwmtydu (Sept–Nov)
viewpoints: Bird's-eye over New Quay harbour; cliff drops at Craig y Delyn
picnic_spots: Cwmtydu cove (pebble) or Pig's Nose headland
food_drink_nearby: The Black Lion New Quay; Harbourmaster Hotel Aberaeron; Cwmtydu Beach Café (seasonal)
hazards_notes: Seal disturbance fines at Cwmtydu during pupping; cliffs crumbly in wet
